title: Adverse weather delaying conductor stringing
description: High winds during autumn/winter months restrict crane and stringing operations on sections at commissioning/main-works stage, particularly on the exposed Leicester Forest East end of the corridor.
mitigation: Build 10% weather contingency into stringing activity durations; maintain a rolling 5-day lookahead against Met Office forecasts.

title: HV cable supply delay from manufacturer
description: Lead times on 275kV cable drums have extended industry-wide; a delay to the current order would push back conductor stringing on later sections.
mitigation: Order placed 6 weeks ahead of need date with buffer stock held at the Northampton laydown area; secondary supplier pre-qualified.

title: Wayleave negotiation delay near Section RA-013
description: Landowner has not yet signed the wayleave agreement for the land parcel Section RA-013 crosses, pending an outstanding ecology survey.
mitigation: Ecology survey rescheduled for next available window; legal team preparing fallback compulsory powers application as a contingency.

title: DNO outage window availability constrained
description: Testing and commissioning activities across multiple sections depend on outage windows granted by the DNO, which has limited availability during winter peak demand.
mitigation: Outage requests submitted 12 weeks ahead per DNO process; monthly coordination call established with DNO planning team.

title: Ecology survey findings requiring redesign
description: A protected species survey on the Rugby-Coventry Spur could require a route micro-siting change if findings are unfavourable.
mitigation: Early engagement with ecologist; design team holds a pre-approved micro-siting corridor of +/-50m to absorb minor route adjustments without a full redesign.

title: Skilled HV testing labour shortage
description: The HV testing team is booked across multiple sections nearing commissioning at the same time; industry-wide shortage of qualified HV test engineers limits ability to bring in additional crews at short notice.
mitigation: Cross-training a second crew now; reviewing sequencing on the Resource Utilisation view to smooth peak demand where the programme allows.

title: Utility diversion (water main) delaying two sections
description: A water main diversion required ahead of foundation works is behind the water company's own programme, currently blocking progress on RA-007 and RB-007.
mitigation: Weekly escalation call with the water company's programme office; considering a temporary works solution to allow foundation works to proceed around the main.

title: Site access track damage from heavy plant
description: Repeated heavy plant movements on the shared farm access track caused surface damage requiring reinstatement, with a near-miss reported for a following vehicle.
mitigation: Track resurfaced with a proper aggregate base; banksman now mandatory for all plant movements on shared farm accesses.

title: Material cost inflation against target cost
description: Steel and copper price volatility could erode the margin under the NEC4 Option C target cost mechanism if sustained through the remaining procurement packages.
mitigation: Key material quantities for remaining sections already procured under fixed-price call-offs; monthly cost report tracks actual vs. target cost pain/gain share.

title: Planning committee delay for Rugby Borough consent
description: The wayleave application for the Rugby Borough Council area is under committee review with no confirmed decision date, which could delay works on RB-006 through RB-009.
mitigation: Pre-application ecology condition being progressed in parallel; planning consultant engaging directly with case officer to seek a committee date.
